segments.text: You step on these every day and they're secretly saving someone life right now.
Tokyo, New York, Mumbai, literally everywhere. So why did the entire world copy something so simple? Japan 1965. A
man's worst fear was coming true. His best friend was losing his sight forever. So he spent everything he had on a crazy idea. She carved a secret
language into the ground. Lines whisper safe. Dots scream stop. It worked. His friend was free again. But then something beautiful happened. Strangers
start following this invisible path. No marketing, no mandate. It just spread.
One street in Japan became 195 countries. The next time you will step on them, you're definitely going to remember this story. For more such amazing design stories, subscribe.
